behaviors.description: The generated function signature matches the expected interface (parameter names, types, return type) specified in the description.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the model’s actual generation quality. A stubbed model can be forced to emit the correct signature and would hide real mismatches. Rejected Workflow: behavior checks one generation outcome, not a multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run live generation from NL specs (typed params + return type) and assert the produced function signature matches exactly; repeat across several specs to expose model variance.

behaviors.description: Orchestration executes attempts in order and returns immediately on the first passing attempt.
behaviors.justification: Primary risk is cross-module orchestration/state handoff (attempt counter, executor result, early-stop). No real OS/network requirement, and correctness does not depend on model output quality if model/test outcomes are deterministic stubs. Rejected Unit: this is not a single-function invariant; it spans orchestrator + executor/client wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Wire orchestrator with deterministic stubs: attempt 1 fail, attempt 2 pass, attempt 3 should not run. Assert call order, stop-on-pass, and returned winning attempt.

behaviors.description: For a function that a single call usually fails, the live iterative loop can still produce a passing implementation within the allowed attempts.
behaviors.justification: Requires multiple real model decisions in sequence (generate -> fail feedback -> refine -> retest) and success depends on live LLM behavior across steps. Any stubbed or single-step test can miss this failure mode.
behaviors.test_description: Use a known prompt where first live attempt often fails; run full live loop with real model and real test execution, assert at least one run passes within <=3 attempts and record attempt progression.

behaviors.description: When tests fail, the refinement prompt builder includes the failed test names and failure messages in the next LLM prompt.
behaviors.justification: Failure appears at module boundary: test-result payload -> prompt-builder content. No need for real model judgment or OS/network. Rejected Agentic: behavior is prompt-content plumbing, not output-quality evaluation.
behaviors.test_description: Feed real-shaped failing test results through orchestration into prompt builder and assert next prompt contains each failed test name and message (including multi-line/escaped text).

behaviors.description: The next live attempt changes implementation content in response to prior failure context rather than resubmitting the same code.
behaviors.justification: This is state handoff across agentic stages: previous failure context must influence a subsequent live model decision. A stub can trivially return different code and hide failure to incorporate context. Rejected Agentic-only: behavior explicitly concerns the next attempt in sequence, not an isolated single call.
behaviors.test_description: Run two-step live loop (attempt 1 fail, attempt 2 refine) with real model; assert attempt-2 code is materially different and specifically addresses prior failure signals (not just random formatting diffs).

behaviors.description: The service stops after 3 failed attempts and returns a structured error with all three attempts and their test results.
behaviors.justification: This is orchestration/bookkeeping across components (loop control + error shaping). Deterministic failing stubs are sufficient; no real infra or model-quality dependency required. Rejected Unit: requires interaction of attempt runner, accumulator, and response formatter.
behaviors.test_description: Configure model/test stubs to fail three times; assert exactly 3 executions, no 4th call, and returned error includes per-attempt code/test-result structure for all attempts.

behaviors.description: The full non-agentic pipeline wiring (generate call, test execution, attempt bookkeeping) works end to end with deterministic stubbed model outputs.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly covers full pipeline including real test execution boundary; these failures often surface only with actual subprocess/filesystem/runtime interactions. Rejected Integration: behavior text requires end-to-end execution path, not just in-process wiring.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end with stubbed model outputs but real code-under-test execution mechanism (same subprocess/runtime path as production); assert generated code is executed, results captured, and attempts tracked correctly.

behaviors.description: The full live loop (generate, test, refine, retest) solves at least one known hard function that fails in the first attempt.
behaviors.justification: Requires multiple sequential live LLM decisions plus real execution feedback loop; correctness is end-to-end agent pipeline behavior. Lower levels cannot expose failures in iterative improvement dynamics.
behaviors.test_description: Use a curated hard task with baseline first-attempt failure; run full live loop with real model and real test run, assert first attempt fails and a later attempt within limit passes.
